---
title: "B | Thema 5"
format: live-html
engine: knitr  
status: "Version 0.1"
lang: de 
---

{{< include _extensions/r-wasm/live/_knitr.qmd >}}
 
## Latente Mittelwertsvergleiche

```{webr}
#| warning: false
#| echo: false

webr::install("quadprog",  repos = "https://kroehne.github.io/webr-quadprog-repo")
 
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(mnormt))
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(lavaan))
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(lavaanPlot))  
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(psych))  
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(DT))

assignInNamespace("lav_options_checkinterval", function(x, nm, num2int) {
    if (any(is.na(x))) return(TRUE)
    if (num2int) x <- as.integer(x)                                                                                              
    oks <- vapply(x, function(x1) {
      (x1 > nm$bounds[1] || (x1 == nm$bounds[1] && nm$first.in)) &&                                                              
      (x1 < nm$bounds[2] || (x1 == nm$bounds[2] && nm$last.in))                                                                  
    }, TRUE)
    all(oks)                                                                                                                     
  }, ns = "lavaan")
 
View <- function(dat){
  dat_name <- deparse(substitute(dat))
  df_display <- dat |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    dplyr::across(everything(), ~ ifelse(is.na(.x), "❌ (NA)", .x))
  )
  
  DT::datatable(
    df_display,
    rownames = FALSE, 
    options = list(
      pageLength = 5,
      scrollX = TRUE,
      dom = 'tip',   
      columnDefs = list(list(className = 'dt-center', targets = "_all"))
    ),
    caption = htmltools::tags$caption(
      style = 'caption-side: top; text-align: left;',
      'data.frame: ', htmltools::strong(dat_name)
    )
  )  
}
```

### Präsentation

```{webr}
#| warning: false
#| echo: true
# ============================================================================
# Thema 05: Latente Mittelwertsvergleiche
# Datensatz: psych::bfi (N=2800, Big Five, Variable gender)
# ============================================================================

library(lavaan)
library(semTools)

data(bfi, package = "psych")
dat <- bfi[complete.cases(bfi[, c(paste0("O", 1:5), "gender")]), ]
dat$gender <- factor(dat$gender, labels = c("Männer", "Frauen"))
dat$O2r <- 7 - dat$O2
dat$O5r <- 7 - dat$O5

# ---- PRÄSENTATION: Latente Mittelwerte Offenheit ----

model_O <- 'Offenheit =~ O1 + O2r + O3 + O4 + O5r'

# Schritt 1: Skalare Invarianz prüfen (Voraussetzung!)
fit_conf <- cfa(model_O, data = dat, group = "gender")
fit_metr <- cfa(model_O, data = dat, group = "gender",
                group.equal = "loadings")
fit_scal <- cfa(model_O, data = dat, group = "gender",
                group.equal = c("loadings", "intercepts"))

# ΔCFI prüfen
cfis <- c(fitMeasures(fit_conf, "cfi"),
          fitMeasures(fit_metr, "cfi"),
          fitMeasures(fit_scal, "cfi"))
cat("CFI (konf/metr/skal):", round(cfis, 4), "\n")
cat("ΔCFI:", round(diff(cfis), 4), "\n\n")

# Schritt 2: Latente Mittelwerte schätzen
# Referenzgruppe (Männer): latenter Mittelwert = 0 (fixiert)
# Andere Gruppe (Frauen): latenter Mittelwert frei geschätzt
fit_means <- cfa(model_O, data = dat, group = "gender",
                 group.equal = c("loadings", "intercepts"),
                 meanstructure = TRUE)
summary(fit_means, standardized = TRUE)

# Latenter Mittelwertsunterschied ablesen
pe <- parameterEstimates(fit_means)
lat_diff <- pe[pe$op == "~1" & pe$lhs == "Offenheit" & pe$group == 2, ]
cat("Latenter MW-Unterschied:\n")
print(lat_diff[, c("est", "se", "z", "pvalue")])

# Effektgröße (Cohen's d auf latenter Ebene)
lat_mean_diff <- lat_diff$est
lat_var_ref <- pe$est[pe$op == "~~" &
  pe$lhs == "Offenheit" & pe$rhs == "Offenheit" &
  pe$group == 1]
d_latent <- lat_mean_diff / sqrt(lat_var_ref)
cat("\nLatentes Cohen's d:", round(d_latent, 3), "\n")

# Vergleich: Manifester t-Test
O_score <- rowMeans(dat[, c("O1", "O2r", "O3", "O4", "O5r")])
t.test(O_score ~ dat$gender)
d_manifest <- diff(tapply(O_score, dat$gender, mean)) / sd(O_score)
cat("Manifestes Cohen's d:", round(d_manifest, 3), "\n")

# Diskrepanz
cat("\nDiskrepanz:", round(abs(d_latent) - abs(d_manifest), 3),
    "(=", round((abs(d_latent)/abs(d_manifest) - 1) * 100, 1), "% größer latent)\n")
cat("→ Manifeste Analyse unterschätzt den Effekt, weil Messfehler den Unterschied abschwächt.\n")
```

## Bericht

```{webr}
#| warning: false
#| echo: true
# ---- BERICHT: Latente Mittelwerte für alle 5 Big-Five-Faktoren ----

# Alle Items umpolen
dat2 <- bfi[complete.cases(bfi[, c(1:25, 26)]), ]
dat2$gender <- factor(dat2$gender, labels = c("Männer", "Frauen"))
dat2$A1r <- 7 - dat2$A1
dat2$C4r <- 7 - dat2$C4; dat2$C5r <- 7 - dat2$C5
dat2$E1r <- 7 - dat2$E1; dat2$E2r <- 7 - dat2$E2
dat2$O2r <- 7 - dat2$O2; dat2$O5r <- 7 - dat2$O5

models_bf <- list(
  A = 'A =~ A1r + A2 + A3 + A4 + A5',
  C = 'C =~ C1 + C2 + C3 + C4r + C5r',
  E = 'E =~ E1r + E2r + E3 + E4 + E5',
  N = 'N =~ N1 + N2 + N3 + N4 + N5',
  O = 'O =~ O1 + O2r + O3 + O4 + O5r'
)

results <- data.frame(Trait = character(), d_latent = numeric(),
                      d_manifest = numeric(), Diskrepanz_pct = numeric(),
                      stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

for (trait in names(models_bf)) {
  items <- all.vars(as.formula(paste("~", gsub(".*=~", "", models_bf[[trait]]))))
  dat_t <- dat2[complete.cases(dat2[, c(items, "gender")]), ]

  fit <- tryCatch(
    cfa(models_bf[[trait]], data = dat_t, group = "gender",
        group.equal = c("loadings", "intercepts"), meanstructure = TRUE),
    error = function(e) NULL)

  if (!is.null(fit)) {
    pe <- parameterEstimates(fit)
    lv_name <- gsub(" =~.*", "", models_bf[[trait]])
    lm_diff <- pe$est[pe$op == "~1" & pe$lhs == lv_name & pe$group == 2]
    lv_var <- pe$est[pe$op == "~~" & pe$lhs == lv_name & pe$rhs == lv_name & pe$group == 1]
    d_lat <- lm_diff / sqrt(lv_var)

    score <- rowMeans(dat_t[, items])
    d_man <- diff(tapply(score, dat_t$gender, mean)) / sd(score)

    diskr <- ifelse(abs(d_man) > 0.01,
                    round((abs(d_lat)/abs(d_man) - 1) * 100, 1), NA)

    results <- rbind(results, data.frame(
      Trait = trait,
      d_latent = round(d_lat, 3),
      d_manifest = round(d_man, 3),
      Diskrepanz_pct = diskr))
  }
}
rownames(results) <- NULL
print(results)

cat("\n→ Bei Offenheit (O) ist die Diskrepanz am größten: Der latente Effekt ist ~45% größer.\n")
cat("→ Bei Extraversion (E) sind d_latent ≈ d_manifest, weil die Ladungen fast tau-äquivalent sind.\n")
cat("→ Faustregel: Je heterogener die Ladungen, desto stärker unterschätzt die manifeste Analyse.\n")

```
